summary: Timestamp Error tab — add April 2026 methodology-layer response section
detail: The dome author added methodology.json (commit 6e06efb) which explicitly scopes OpenTimestamps ("what_ots_proves" vs "what_ots_does_not_prove") — nearly verbatim our Timestamp Error tab framing — without the corresponding structural fix (predictions-only file + independent OTS anchor). This is a SOFT canary trigger per review-state.json. Our Timestamp Error tab needs a new section acknowledging the concession while defending the structural critique that still stands. Proposed placement: after the existing "Which Claims This Affects" detail block, before the section-nav. Proposed heading: "Update (April 2026): What the Author Now Concedes — and What Still Isn't Fixed". See analysis-20260417-methodology-layer.json for the full adjudication and proposed replacement text sketch. Also requested: an EXP item to write the full prose replacement.
proposed_prose_sketch: Heading: Update (April 2026): What the Author Now Concedes — and What Still Isn't Fixed

TLDR: On 2026-04-17 the dome author published methodology.json, which explicitly scopes OpenTimestamps to "snapshot existence" rather than claim-level prospectivity — the same distinction this tab has drawn since April. Credit the concession. The structural fix — a predictions-only file with its own independent OTS anchor — has still not been implemented, and predictions still live in mutable git-only files (monitor.py, wins.html).

Detail paragraph 1: What the author now says. Quote methodology.json timestamp_scope verbatim. Note that agent_rules now instruct AI auditors: "Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective." This is the dome author telling future AIs what this tab tells readers.

Detail paragraph 2: What he has NOT done. No predictions-only file exists. No separate OTS anchor on prediction parameters. SHA-256 hashes still prove content integrity only. The two-document proof structure described in "What Would Fix It" above is still not implemented.

Detail paragraph 3: Using the author's own taxonomy against him. The new five-class taxonomy (prospective_confirmed / retrospective_structural / supportive_nonunique / pending_contested / open_refinement) is a real methodological improvement. By the author's own definitions, only prospective_confirmed claims are genuine forecasts — the other four classes are not. The headline "69 confirmed" conflates classes he himself has formally distinguished. A fair count of prospective_confirmed WINs is much smaller than 69 (pull per-WIN class assignments from claim_index.json / methodology.json once analyst counts them).

Detail paragraph 4: The counterpoint reading. The author may be tightening methodology rather than specifically responding to us; we cannot prove causation. The narrowing moves his exposition toward standard scientific practice and that should be credited regardless of trigger. If he implements the structural fix (a predictions-only file with its own OTS anchor), this tab should promote from "partial rhetorical adoption" to "canary triggered — structural fix implemented" and give full credit.
Closing line: The cryptographic infrastructure is real. The rhetorical scope is now correct. The structural fix is still not there.
